messages.content:
CVE-2009-10002 is a medium-severity cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability found in the dpup fittr-flickr application, specifically within the EXIF Preview Handler component. The issue arises from inadequate neutralization of user-controllable input in the file fittr-flickr/features/easy-exif.js, which can lead to XSS attacks. This vulnerability can be exploited remotely, allowing attackers to execute scripts in the context of the user's browser.

The Common Weakness Enumeration (CWE) associated with this vulnerability is CWE-79, which involves improper input neutralization before using it in web pages served to users. The affected versions of the fittr-flickr software before November 3, 2009, are particularly susceptible.

The associated MITRE ATT&CK technique is T1190. To mitigate this issue, it is recommended to apply the patch with the identifier 08875dd8a2e5d0d16568bb0d67cb4328062fccde. Additional mitigation techniques include employing web application firewalls (WAFs) and ensuring input validation and output encoding are robust. Applying these measures can significantly reduce the risk of exploitation.
